suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note under Implementation Guidance that the session GET handler uses fire-and-forget side-effects (hydration, discovery, preparation) as an established pattern in this V1 codebase — future workers should be aware that GET /api/sessions/[sessionId] is not side-effect-free and should preserve idempotency guards when extending it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature auto-start-single-video-preparation-followup added prepareItems() as a fire-and-forget call inside the GET handler (app/api/sessions/[sessionId]/route.ts:53), extending the existing pattern where the same GET handler already calls hydrateSingleVideoSession() and discoverAndHydrateSession(). The review noted this is architecturally different from triggering preparation from a POST handler but is consistent with the existing codebase convention.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the Known Test Stability Notes to include tests/api/single-video-intake.test.ts alongside session-prepare.route.test.ts and workflow-gating.test.ts as files using the 400ms afterEach cleanup delay pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature auto-start-single-video-preparation-followup added a 400ms afterEach delay in tests/api/single-video-intake.test.ts:28, matching the same cleanup timing pattern documented for two other test files. AGENTS.md should mention all three files that use this pattern.
